title: This submission is a formal follow-up/addendum regarding open Public Records Request #R007027-040726 and FOIA Appeal 2026-209

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

VIA EMAIL

To: Mayor's Office of Legal Counsel (MOLC)
Cc: M. Claudine Sassa, Senior Paralegal & FOIA Officer, DISB
Cc: Lilah Blackstone, DISB Acting General Counsel

Subject: REBUTTAL TO DISB SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE – FOIA Appeal 2026-209 (Public Records #R007027-040726)

Dear Mayor's Office of Legal Counsel,

I am in receipt of the Department of Insurance, Securities and Banking’s (DISB) supplemental response dated June 5, 2026, regarding FOIA Appeal 2026-209. DISB’s continued reliance on D.C. Official Code § 2-534(a)(1) ("Exemption 1") to withhold 92 pages of records is a gross misapplication of the statute and fails to satisfy the burden of proof required by your June 1, 2026, remand instructions.

DISB claims the withheld records consist of "detailed line-item invoices and accounting summaries" pertaining to Housing Counseling Services, and that these documents constitute exempt "trade secrets" or confidential commercial information. This assertion is fundamentally flawed for the following reasons:

1. Misapplication of the "Trade Secret" Standard
DISB cites Public Citizen Health Research Group v. FDA, 704 F.2d 1280 (D.C. Cir. 1983), defining a trade secret as "a secret, commercially valuable plan, formula, process, or device." Standard line-item invoices, accounting summaries, and legal fee structures utilized by a housing counseling provider do not constitute an "end product of either innovation or substantial effort" under this strict definition. Billing rates and service descriptions are administrative records, not proprietary formulas or scientific processes. 

2. Failure to Demonstrate Actual Competitive Harm
Under Exemption 1, an agency cannot rely on conclusory or boilerplate assertions of competitive harm. DISB states that disclosure "would violate statutory protections and harm the provider's competitive interests" but offers zero factual explanation as to how revealing standard accounting summaries for housing and foreclosure mediation services would cause substantial competitive injury to Housing Counseling Services. 

3. Public Interest and Accountability
The records in question relate to services rendered during a foreclosure mediation process initiated via a referral from the DISB Foreclosure Ombudsman. There is a superseding public interest in the transparency of how legal fees and accounting summaries are generated and billed by entities handling consumer foreclosure mediations under the auspices of a District regulatory agency.

DISB has failed to adequately explain how standard invoices fall within the narrow scope of D.C. Code § 2-534(a)(1). I respectfully request that the MOLC order the immediate release of the 92 pages, with only narrowly tailored redactions applied to specific, genuinely proprietary data, rather than permitting DISB to execute a blanket withholding of administrative accounting records.

Sincerely,

Fred A. Gamble Jr.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

FRED GAMBLE JR

title: Public Information Request: FY24 SB224 Catalytic Converter 2nd Solicitation (Refugio Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Public Information Request: FY24 SB224 Catalytic Converter 2nd Solicitation (Refugio Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all documents, including but not limited to, applications, agreements, and reporting documents related to the award for the FY24 SB224 Catalytic Converter 2nd Solicitation administered by the Motor Vehicle Crime Prevention Authority.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: Public Information Request: FY24 SB224 Catalytic Converter 2nd Solicitation (Refugio Police Department)
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon.
 
We never received monies from this grant.  Yes we did apply, and got approved for the grant, but the Chief at the time pull the department from the grant.
 
 
Debbie Stephens
Administrative Assistant
Refugio Police Department
361-526-4533
